Notice Of Motion Setting Compensation Of Video Recorded Expert Deposition
Off Calendar Jul-27-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Atty. Albert L. Thuesen III, Esq. of Coit Law Group (Tel: 415-787-2648) for plaintiff Phuong Hoang;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF PHUONG HOANG'S Motion Setting Compensation Of Video Recorded Expert Deposition.

Plaintiff's counsel stipulates to the matter being decided by the judge pro tem. The judge pro tem and plaintiff's counsel both appearing remotely authorize the clerk to sign the stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling: Plaintiff has deemed the videotaped deposition rate of expert witness Dr. William Hoddick to be unreasonable and moved for an order setting compensation of Dr. Hoddick's video recorded expert deposition pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Section 2034.470. The court finds that neither Dr. Hoddick nor the party designating him as an expert has submitted admissible evidence under Section 2034.470 sufficient to establish the ordinary and customary fee actually charged by Dr. Hoddick for similar services provided outside the subject litigation. It is undisputed that Dr. Hoddick's rate for a transcribed deposition is $750 per hour and that plaintiff does not object to paying that rate. It is further undisputed that the deposition is to be taken via Zoom. The Court issues an interim order that Dr. Hoddick shall appear for deposition before a certified shorthand reporter via Zoom and that Dr. Hoddick shall be compensated at the rate of $750 per hour. The Zoom session may be recorded and used as a videotape at trial. In the event that Dr. Hoddick contends in retrospect that the compensation paid for his deposition has been insufficient, the court will entertain an application on his behalf to amend the rate set in the interim order. Such application must be supported by admissible evidence consistent with Code of Civil Procedure Section 2034.470. Unless such an application is filed within thirty days after the completion of the deposition, the interim order will become final. The Court will further issue a protective order to preclude improper use of the Zoom recording. Counsel are ordered to meet and confer as to the terms of the protective order. Dr. Hoddick or counsel on his behalf may participate in the drafting of the protective order. Counsel shall submit the proposed protective order in a single document in Word format within seven days of the date of the interim order. Any disputes as the language or terms of the protective order shall be highlighted in the proposal. The Court finds that the imposition of sanctions would be unjust under the circumstances of this case.

Plaintiff's counsel is to submit a proposed order in Word format to the judge pro tem.

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
